Building · Mother’s Day email
Today · 10:22 AM
Make the Mother’s Day one warmer. Less about premium positioning, more about the relationship.10:22
Magellan · 10:23
Updated. The opener now leads with the customers who’ve ordered three Mother’s Days running — 23%of last year’s buyers are repeat. Premium framing is still there, underneath rather than on top.
Good. The opener works. Swap the hero image to something warmer — current lavender feels editorial.10:25
Magellan · 10:25
Switched to a softer dawn palette — warm pinks and apricots pulled from your wedding catalog. The selected one reads as familiar rather than aspirational.
Magellan · 10:27
I think it’s ready. Set to reach 2,847 repeat Mother’s Day customers, Sun May 11 at 8:00 AM, anchored at $2,100 to hold the premium read.

Push to Bridge whenever you want and I’ll line it up for your final approval. Nothing ships until you say so.
Scoped to Mother’s Day email
Email · Editing · auto-saved 10:27
Three Mother’s Days in a row — and counting
Three Mother’s Days in a row — and counting
InboxMother’s Day
U
UrbanFlowers Bangkok to you
hello@urbanflowers.co.th · May 11, 8:00 AM
For Mother’s Day · May 11
Hello, again.
Three years ago, 184 of you ordered flowers for your mother for the first time. Last year, you came back. The year before that too.
We notice these things. Not because we track customers — we don’t really think of you that way — but because three Mother’s Days in a row is its own kind of relationship. With her. With us. With the idea that some things deserve to become tradition.
This year we’ve put together three arrangements for the people who’ve shown up again and again. Each references something we’ve learned about what your mothers love — softer palettes, longer-lasting blooms, the small detail that makes it feel like your bouquet, not a bouquet.
See the three arrangements →
Thank you for letting us be part of your tradition.
— Johannes & the UrbanFlowers team
UrbanFlowers Bangkok
89/12 Charoenkrung Soi 30, Bangrak, Bangkok 10500
